mission.title: Install and Configure Armada on Kubernetes
mission.description: Armada is a CNCF sandbox project for multi-cluster batch job scheduling. It enables efficient queuing, scheduling, and management of high-throughput compute workloads across multiple Kubernetes clusters, ideal for enterprises with large-scale batch processing needs.

steps.title: Install prerequisites
steps.description:
Armada requires several dependencies to be installed first. Ensure the following are running in your cluster:

- **PostgreSQL** — persistent storage for job metadata
- **Redis** — caching layer
- **Apache Pulsar** — message broker for inter-component communication

You can install these via Helm:
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm install postgresql bitnami/postgresql --namespace armada --create-namespace
helm install redis bitnami/redis --namespace armada
helm repo add apache https://pulsar.apache.org/charts
helm install pulsar apache/pulsar --namespace armada
```

steps.title: Add the Armada Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the G-Research Helm repository:
```bash
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Armada server using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Armada server. You will need to configure the values to point to your PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar instances:
```bash
helm install armada-server gresearch/armada --namespace armada --version 0.20.36
```

For a complete deployment, you also need the executor (per worker cluster) and scheduler:
```bash
helm install armada-executor gresearch/armada-executor --namespace armada
helm install armada-scheduler gresearch/armada-scheduler --namespace armada
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Armada components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

You should see pods for armada-server, armada-executor, and armada-scheduler.

resolution.summary: A successful installation shows the Armada server, executor, and scheduler pods running in the 'armada' namespace, with connectivity to PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove Armada Helm releases
uninstall.description:
Uninstall all Armada components:
```bash
helm uninstall armada-server --namespace armada
helm uninstall armada-executor --namespace armada
helm uninstall armada-scheduler --namespace armada
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace and all remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace armada
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the repository to get the latest chart versions:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Armada
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Armada server:
```bash
helm upgrade armada-server gresearch/armada --namespace armada
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check pod status after upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Usually caused by missing or misconfigured dependencies. Check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada
```

Verify that PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar are running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Database connection errors
troubleshooting.description:
Verify PostgreSQL is running and the connection string is correct:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep postgresql
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada | grep -i database
```

troubleshooting.title: Pulsar connection issues
troubleshooting.description:
Verify Pulsar is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep pulsar
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Helm and kubectl. Armada depends on PostgreSQL, Redis, and Apache Pulsar, which must be installed before deploying Armada.
